items.headline: Major commercial project approved on West San Carlos Street
items.summary: A $696,570 commercial/industrial development is underway at 1300 W San Carlos St, representing significant economic activity in San José's commercial corridor. This is the largest project in the recent permit batch and suggests notable business expansion or infrastructure investment in the area.

items.headline: Cupertino launches business license amnesty program for shopping centers
items.summary: The city is offering a Business License Amnesty Program targeting shopping center businesses, likely allowing unpaid or unlicensed operators to get current without penalties. This could affect local retail and small businesses throughout Cupertino's commercial districts.

items.headline: City rolling out 'Summer of Celebrations' local shopping campaign
items.summary: Cupertino is launching a promotional campaign to encourage residents to shop locally, part of broader economic development efforts supporting retail and small businesses in the area.

items.headline: Great American Litter Pick-up designated annual city-sponsored event
items.summary: The council approved making the Great American Litter Pick-up an official annual city-sponsored event and authorized spending city funds and accepting donations. This formalization supports regular community cleanup efforts.

items.headline: New retail vitality policies coming to Palo Alto zoning code
items.summary: The Planning Commission is considering changes to zoning and building regulations to implement retail vitality policies from the city's Comprehensive Plan. This could affect how retail spaces are designed and operated across Palo Alto.

items.headline: San Antonio Road area plan study under way
items.summary: The Planning Commission held a study session on the San Antonio Road Area Plan, examining land use and transportation alternatives for this corridor. This planning effort could shape future development and traffic patterns in that area.

items.headline: Los Altos adopts new Below Market Rate housing transfer rules
items.summary: The city council approved a resolution modifying exemptions for Below Market Rate (BMR) unit transfers, potentially affecting affordable housing policies and eligibility requirements for current and future BMR residents in Los Altos.

items.headline: City council establishes formal norms and procedures for meetings
items.summary: Los Altos adopted official City Council Norms and Procedures on March 24, establishing formal guidelines for how council meetings will be conducted and decisions made going forward.
